steps.title: Find the missing angle in each triangle
steps.content: In any triangle, the sum of interior angles is 180°. So for triangle DEF, $$m\angle F = 180^\circ - (43^\circ + 112^\circ) = 25^\circ.$$ Similarly, for triangle RST, $$m\angle T = 180^\circ - (43^\circ + 112^\circ) = 25^\circ.$$ Thus both triangles have angles 43°, 112°, and 25°.

steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content: Since two angles of triangle DEF equal two angles of triangle RST (∠D = ∠R = 43° and ∠E = ∠S = 112°), the triangles are similar by the Angle‑Angle (AA) criterion. Therefore no additional information is required to establish similarity. That matches choice D — D is correct.
